response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong fundamentals — TDD, CI/CD, Linux, distributed/microservices work at Sociomantic handling millions of req/s, and geospatial domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role demands modern C++ (or Rust) as the primary language, and the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments back in 2014-2015. Primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no Rust and no recent C++ at scale. Geospatial background and willingness to learn across the stack are real positives, but the core language gap is significant for a Senior IC routing-tiling role.
strengths: Strong geospatial/GIS domain experience (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G talk)
Low-latency distributed-systems work at Sociomantic (millions of req/s, async I/O, fibers)
Engineering rigor: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tooling authorship
concerns: Minimal and dated C++ exposure (NI, 2014-2015); no Rust experience listed
Recent work is TypeScript/Node-heavy, not systems-level performance code
